manualTest: Production artifact topology is unambiguous
steps: Compare the release workflow, published asset names, bundle identifier, update feeds, release notes, and locked/entitled test instructions.
expected: Every source describes one production Pro-capable artifact that starts in locked Core state and activates Pro only after entitlement; local Core/Pro builds are labeled diagnostic.
proof: The workflow currently describes one signed Pro-capable DMG while older release documentation used a two-artifact vocabulary.
remaining: Resolve the release topology and verify the exact published assets, feeds, bundle ID, and checklist use the same model.
notes: Audit gap: contradictory release topology can invalidate otherwise correct QA evidence.

manualTest: Final downloaded macOS artifact passes Apple trust checks
steps: On a clean quarantined Mac, download the final DMG and run strict deep signature verification, Team ID and hardened-runtime inspection, notarization staple validation, Gatekeeper assessment, and ASAR fuse inspection.
expected: The exact downloaded app is Developer ID-signed by Team 84V6KCAC49, notarized, stapled, Gatekeeper-accepted, and enforces both ASAR integrity fuses.
proof: The trust seam requires strict signature, fuse, Developer ID, staple, and Gatekeeper checks and rejects disabled ASAR fuses.
remaining: Repeat the checks on the exact downloaded production artifact and a clean Mac against Apple services.

manualTest: Detached DMG installation is self-contained
steps: Confirm the DMG has exactly one app and the Applications alias, copy the app to /Applications, eject the DMG, disconnect build volumes, then launch.
expected: The installed app starts with no dependency on the mounted image, repository, temporary build paths, or a second nested app.
proof: Automation copies out of a mounted DMG, detaches, launches, rejects two-app DMGs, and verifies strict signature under the trust gate.
remaining: Repeat with Finder and the exact final downloaded DMG on the release Mac.

manualTest: Every packaged native helper executes in production conditions
steps: From the installed app, exercise llama-server, Whisper/Parakeet, ffmpeg, image runtime, TTS, dictation, and meeting helpers with Homebrew and repository paths unavailable.
expected: Every shipped helper executes without missing @rpath libraries, temporary build paths, foreign dependencies, unsupported minOS, or silent System Health mismatch.
proof: Package gates inspect integrity and dependencies and execute the packaged helper set, including real TTS synthesis.
remaining: Execute the exact final app on target hardware and reconcile each result with installed System Health.
notes: Release blocker; catches the failure class that broke earlier releases.

manualTest: Supported macOS and architecture matrix executes
steps: Install and run every nested executable on the oldest and newest supported macOS; record Apple Silicon and Intel/Rosetta policy and result.
expected: The app and every helper start on each advertised OS/architecture with no deployment-target or architecture mismatch.
proof: Build gates inspect minimum macOS metadata and helper architecture/dependency closure.
remaining: Run the physical supported OS and architecture matrix.
notes: Release policy must explicitly state unsupported architectures.

manualTest: Published-feed upgrade preserves Core and Pro state
steps: Upgrade through the real stable feed from the previous stable locked Core state and a previous Pro-entitled state, then restart into 0.0.40.
expected: Chats, projects, models, settings, permissions, encrypted data, Pro stores, and entitlement remain intact and the new version is running.
proof: Updater state, release assets, schema owners, and representative prior-profile migrations are automated separately.
remaining: Join them through the real CDN/feed, signed ZIP, OS restart, Keychain, and installed application.
notes: Run both locked and entitled upgrade fixtures.

manualTest: Complete legacy profiles migrate without silent omission
steps: Back up and upgrade representative full profiles containing encrypted DB data, license cache, OAuth tokens, models, chats, projects, captures, entities, meetings, clipboard, and vault data.
expected: No onboarding reset, Keychain loss, duplicate records, missing category, or cross-tier activation occurs; all data owners reopen correctly.
proof: Pinned legacy schemas migrate through real Core and Pro database owners.
remaining: Upgrade complete real prior-version profiles through the installed artifact and macOS Keychain.

manualTest: Interrupted migration is recoverable and idempotent
steps: Terminate the app at controlled points during schema migration, then relaunch repeatedly against a backed-up disposable legacy profile.
expected: The prior profile remains usable or is restored safely; retry completes once with no partial schema, duplicate records, or destructive loop.
proof: The production CRM migration now runs as one SQLite transaction. A real-disk integration forces SQLITE_FULL mid-migration, proves the transaction rolls back without losing the legacy row or leaking partial schema, then closes and reopens twice to prove retry, idempotence, and no duplicate observations.
remaining: Repeat with literal process termination during migration against a backed-up installed legacy profile.
notes: Release blocker for destructive migration failures.

manualTest: Active TCC revocation stops collection immediately
steps: While capture, meeting recording, and dictation are active, revoke Screen Recording, Microphone, and Accessibility one at a time, then regrant and retry.
expected: Collection and paste stop immediately, errors are specific, no stale helper continues, and regrant recovers without prompt or relaunch loops beyond macOS requirements.
proof: The real focus scheduler, capture loop, persisted settings, and capture IPC run against a disposable profile. Active Screen Recording revocation stops frames and further focus-boundary calls, reports an actionable denied status, and automatically resumes after regrant. Microphone and Accessibility denial remain covered at their production integration seams.
remaining: Revoke each TCC permission against the signed app while the actual macOS capture, audio, and paste resources are live.

manualTest: Lock, sleep, and wake do not capture or orphan resources
steps: Lock and sleep the Mac during capture, meeting recording, and dictation; wait; wake and unlock; inspect data, helpers, indicators, and recovery.
expected: Nothing is captured while locked, no helper or mic indicator is orphaned, partial data is reconciled, and wake resumes only when user settings allow.
proof: The real persisted capture owner and production Pro composition root now receive lock, unlock, suspend, and resume events through one lifecycle coordinator. Integration coverage proves overlapping lock plus sleep cannot resume early, meetings are stopped once, dictation ownership is suspended, and a persisted user pause survives wake.
remaining: Run real macOS power and session events on the release device.

manualTest: Multi-display capture preserves display privacy
steps: Use two displays with different scale/orientation, place sensitive content on the secondary display, then connect, disconnect, and rearrange displays while capture is active.
expected: Only intended displays/surfaces are captured, exclusions apply everywhere, no stale or wrong-display frames leak, and capture recovers after topology changes.
evidence: No ScreenCaptureKit multi-display topology journey was found.
proof: None for this failure class.
remaining: Exercise real displays, scaling, orientation, and hot-plug behavior.

manualTest: Gateway is loopback-only or explicitly authenticated
steps: With the app running, probe every gateway route from localhost and a second LAN device across Wi-Fi/Ethernet and firewall changes.
expected: Non-loopback access is rejected, or intentional LAN access has explicit authentication, disclosure, and a tested threat model; docs match the bind behavior.
proof: The production gateway binds to the canonical 127.0.0.1 host. A real-socket integration proves loopback HTTP succeeds and a TCP connection through the Mac's non-loopback IPv4 interface is rejected; API docs, renderer copy, and CSP reuse the same host constant.
remaining: Confirm the installed 0.0.40 app remains unreachable from a second LAN device across Wi-Fi and Ethernet changes.
notes: P0 network exposure is fixed; retain the installed-device probe as release evidence.

manualTest: OAuth callbacks enforce exact state and lifecycle
steps: Exercise correct, absent, wrong, and replayed state; cancel/error; timeout; two concurrent flows; callback-port collision; and quit/relaunch.
expected: Only the exact pending state can receive tokens; every invalid, stale, replayed, or unrelated callback fails closed without persisting credentials.
proof: Twelve real loopback HTTP integration cases prove missing, wrong, duplicate, expired, replayed, malformed, provider-error, and concurrent callback states fail closed or route only to their exact pending request. The listener is confirmed bound before the browser opens, retry cancels only its prior flow, and state is removed before settlement.
remaining: Confirm the same callback lifecycle once with a real system browser and OAuth provider on the installed 0.0.40 app.
notes: P0 callback-state admission is fixed; retain one real-provider pass as release evidence.

manualTest: Local-only product journeys create no undeclared egress
steps: Run chat, OCR, image, audio, capture, entity, search, and vault journeys behind packet capture with downloads, updates, licensing, and connectors idle.
expected: No DNS or network egress occurs except an action the tester explicitly initiates and the UI clearly identifies.
evidence: Narrow offline and boundary tests exist; no packet-level product-wide egress test was found.
proof: None for the complete advertised local-only guarantee.
remaining: Inspect DNS and network traffic for all local feature families on the release build.

manualTest: Diagnostics are useful and redact every private payload
steps: Inject synthetic bearer tokens, API keys, passwords, prompts, filenames, transcripts, connector data, and helper failures; inspect active and rotated logs plus Console output and file permissions.
expected: Secrets and business payloads are absent, log files remain private and bounded, and each failure still has a readable operation, state, duration, and safe reason.
proof: Automation covers one-line logging, credential redaction, private file mode, lifecycle events, and omission of request bodies.
remaining: Inspect real helper stderr, all Core/Pro business flows, Console, and rotated files in the installed app.

manualTest: Force quit during native collection reconciles partial work
steps: Force quit or simulate power loss during meeting recording, dictation, capture write, and transcription; relaunch and inspect data and processes.
expected: Partial media is recovered or clearly marked, orphan helpers are adopted or stopped, no recording restarts silently, and finalized records are neither lost nor duplicated.
proof: Persistence and ownership recovery are covered at controlled process/native boundaries.
remaining: Kill the actual installed process during physical audio/capture and inspect filesystem durability.

manualTest: Windows publication decision and evidence are explicit
steps: Inspect the release workflow and final assets; if Windows ships, run signed install, lock/entitlement, native-helper, update, upgrade, and uninstall tests on supported Windows; otherwise prevent asset publication.
expected: No untested Windows artifact is published and the release notes state the supported platform set accurately.
evidence: .github/workflows/release.yml contains a Windows build while the manual document makes Windows conditional.
proof: No final Windows device evidence is recorded in this checklist.
remaining: Make and enforce the release decision, then attach Windows evidence when applicable.
notes: Release blocker if a Windows asset will be published.

manualTest: Stable and beta channel transitions select safe artifacts
steps: Switch stable to beta and beta to stable across versions, including any allowDowngrade path; check feed URL, offered version, restart, and schema compatibility.
expected: The app selects only the chosen channel, never applies an unsafe downgrade, and preserves data and channel choice after restart.
proof: Update state and channel persistence are automated; updater configuration permits downgrade.
remaining: Exercise published stable/beta feeds and real version ordering with signed artifacts.
notes: Never use real personal data for downgrade testing.

manualTest: Interrupted update download leaves a launchable app
steps: During an update download, cut network, fill a disposable volume, quit, and retry; inspect prompts, cache, current installation, and subsequent update.
expected: The current app stays launchable, partial data is cleaned or safely resumed, retries do not loop or duplicate, and no install runs without approval.
proof: Offline, timeout, retry, staging, and approval state are covered at the updater boundary.
remaining: Exercise Electron updater cache, real network interruption, and disk pressure.

manualTest: Uninstall and reinstall follow the documented data policy
steps: Remove only the app bundle and reinstall, then separately run Delete All before reinstall; inspect userData, Keychain, connector secrets, license state, and personal records.
expected: Bundle-only uninstall retains data as documented; explicit Delete All prevents personal data or credentials from reappearing after reinstall.
evidence: Core and Pro delete-all and secret-deletion integration tests
proof: In-app deletion owners are tested, but Finder uninstall/reinstall and Keychain policy are not joined.
remaining: Perform actual bundle removal, reinstall, and Keychain/userData inspection.
notes: Back up any non-disposable profile first.

manualTest: Global shortcut collisions and keyboard changes recover
steps: Create a shortcut collision with another app, switch keyboard layout/remapping, and change dictation mode while a key is held.
expected: Unavailable registration is visible, a usable fallback remains, settings do not create duplicate events, and restart/rebind is clean.
proof: Shortcut ownership, parsing, rebind, and teardown are covered with the OS registration boundary controlled.
remaining: Use real globalShortcut, layouts/remappers, and a competing application.

manualTest: Microphone route changes do not strand recording
steps: Start dictation and meeting recording, then unplug, replace, or switch the default mic, including Bluetooth/AirPods and no-input states.
expected: The app preserves or clearly fails partial recording, releases the old route and mic indicator, selects the new route when possible, and can record again.
evidence: Voice and meeting integrations control the native audio boundary.
proof: Product state is tested, not CoreAudio device churn.
remaining: Exercise physical and Bluetooth input changes on the release Mac.

manualTest: Meeting audio sources and timing remain truthful
steps: Record system audio plus mic across mute, silence, headphones, and differing sample rates/channels; inspect waveform, duration, transcript timing, and attribution.
expected: Audio is not duplicated or fed back, mute/silence is represented honestly, timing remains aligned, and the summary uses the complete intended recording.
proof: Persistence, duration, transcription selection, and lifecycle are tested with native audio controlled.
remaining: Record through real system audio, mic routes, devices, and sample formats.

manualTest: Connector credential revocation and deletion are complete
steps: Complete OAuth, inspect profile/database/logs for plaintext tokens, revoke remotely, delete the connector, restart, then reconnect.
expected: Tokens remain encrypted and redacted, revocation becomes an actionable error, deletion removes every owned secret, and deleted credentials never return silently.
proof: Production encrypted persistence and owned-secret deletion are covered with the provider boundary controlled.
remaining: Use a real OAuth provider, macOS protected storage, remote revocation, and reconnect.

manualTest: Network middleboxes fail without corrupting local state
steps: Exercise proxy, VPN, captive portal, DNS failure, and TLS interception for downloads, connectors, licensing, and updates while also using local features.
expected: Local features remain usable, each network failure is specific, retries do not duplicate or leak state, and no insecure TLS fallback occurs.
evidence: Update offline/timeout tests, connector timeout tests, download integrity tests, and licensing boundary tests
proof: Individual timeout/error owners are covered; OS network configurations are not.
remaining: Use real proxy/VPN/captive/DNS/TLS conditions and provider endpoints.
notes: Never install an untrusted root on a primary machine.

manualTest: Native notification permission and stale targets recover
steps: Deny then grant Notifications, click meeting/approval/to-do notifications, click a stale target, and change notification settings while delivery is pending.
expected: Delivery follows permission, clicks focus the app and open the exact live record, stale targets fail closed with a recoverable fallback, and no duplicate notification appears.
proof: Typed targets and rendered routing are covered separately from macOS Notification Center.
remaining: Exercise real notification permission, delivery, click/focus, and stale records.

manualTest: VoiceOver can complete critical journeys
steps: Use VoiceOver for onboarding, model installation, chat, connector approval, Settings, destructive confirmations, and every Pro navigation surface.
expected: Controls have useful names/roles, status changes announce, reading order is logical, dialogs trap and restore focus, and critical actions are possible without sight.
evidence: DOM role/focus tests exist but no VoiceOver or macOS accessibility-tree automation was found.
proof: Keyboard focus styling does not prove screen-reader usability.
remaining: Perform a real VoiceOver pass on the installed app.

manualTest: Native app-window lifecycle follows macOS conventions
steps: Exercise Cmd+W, Cmd+Q, Cmd+H, Dock reopen, app activation with no windows, About, Settings, and reopening after the last window closes.
expected: Window and process lifecycle are consistent, data is committed, the app can be reopened, and no unintended background capture or model process remains.
proof: Quit/relaunch and owned-resource teardown have automation, but native menu/window conventions do not.
remaining: Use macOS menu, Dock, keyboard, Activity Monitor, indicators, and ports.
notes: Also run the P0 clean-shutdown row below.

manualTest: macOS contrast, transparency, keyboard, and zoom settings remain usable
steps: Enable Increase Contrast, Reduce Transparency, Full Keyboard Access, and supported text/page zoom across dense lists, dialogs, charts, and side panels.
expected: Text, focus, controls, and hierarchy remain legible; no clipping, hidden actions, translucent blanks, or unreachable content appears.
evidence: e2e/desktop-polish.spec.ts covers keyboard focus and reduced motion only.
proof: Adjacent desktop accessibility behavior is automated; these OS settings are not.
remaining: Inspect pixels and keyboard access under each macOS setting.

manualTest: Diagnostics failure and rotation do not destabilize the app
steps: Make the diagnostics directory unwritable and use a disposable full volume while producing enough synthetic events to rotate logs.
expected: The app continues safely, reports diagnostics failure without recursion, keeps bounded storage, and does not lose unrelated product data.
proof: Logger behavior, redaction, mode, and some write failure handling are automated.
remaining: Exercise actual filesystem permissions, rotation, and full-disk behavior.

manualTest: TTS output-device changes recover
steps: Play speech, switch output device and volume, disconnect Bluetooth audio, press Stop, reconnect, and synthesize again.
expected: Playback stops promptly, no old audio continues, device loss is visible, resources release, and the next synthesis plays through the active output.
proof: Synthesis, TTS/STT round trip, rendered stop state, and package execution are covered separately.
remaining: Exercise real CoreAudio output routing and physical/Bluetooth devices.

manualTest: Window returns on-screen after display topology changes
steps: Place the app and transient windows on an external display, disconnect it, change clamshell state, relaunch, and reopen dialogs/popups.
expected: Every window returns on-screen with sensible size, focus, stacking, and no invisible modal or lost popup.
evidence: No AppKit display-topology/window-placement journey was found.
proof: None for this OS boundary.
remaining: Use a real external display and clamshell transitions.
notes: Check dictation and clipboard popup windows too.

manualTest: Unicode and space-containing paths work
steps: Install and run from paths containing spaces and use a disposable macOS account with a non-ASCII username; download/import models and create logs/artifacts.
expected: Models, helpers, logs, database, updates, exports, and native processes work without shell-escaping or path-encoding failures.
evidence: Packaged-helper tests cover an application path with spaces but not a Unicode home directory.
proof: Space handling is partially covered.
remaining: Run under a real Unicode account/home path.

manualTest: Timezone, locale clock, and DST changes preserve chronology
steps: Change timezone and 12/24-hour preference while running; use synthetic timestamps around a DST boundary; inspect meetings, capture, Day, retention, and filenames.
expected: Absolute order and retention remain correct, labels update truthfully, and no event is duplicated, skipped, or assigned to the wrong day.
evidence: Reflect/time-range and persistence tests cover narrower fixed-time cases.
proof: Time calculations have integration evidence, but live OS timezone/DST changes do not.
remaining: Exercise actual timezone and locale changes with the installed app.

manualTest: Live light/dark appearance changes keep every layer legible
steps: Switch macOS appearance while dialogs, menus, lightboxes, side panels, native prompts, and popup windows are open.
expected: Theme, title bar, focus, icons, contrast, overlays, and native integration update without restart, blank content, or unreadable combinations.
proof: Theme selection logic is covered; live multi-window pixels are not.
remaining: Inspect every window and transient layer during real macOS appearance changes.

phase: 19 Pro entity, action, and service guardrails
manualTest: No Pro background service activates before entitlement
steps: Start the production app on a fresh unentitled profile and watch processes, global shortcuts, clipboard, notifications, capture, meetings, tray, media ports, and Pro database writes; then activate and restart.
expected: Before entitlement no Pro service, shortcut, watcher, capture, or write activates; after entitlement each service starts once under its owning lifecycle.
proof: Service activation and source separation are covered at application seams.
remaining: Observe the exact installed locked and entitled artifact against real OS services.

phase: 19 Pro entity, action, and service guardrails
manualTest: Manual to-do creation enriches context without pollution
steps: Jot a synthetic to-do naming a known entity, implied priority, and due date; wait for enrichment; quit and reopen.
expected: The to-do appears immediately, enrichment terminates, preserves the original intent, adds justified entity/date/priority/source context, creates no duplicate entity, and survives relaunch.
layer: Rendered integration + real SQLite + local HTTP
proof: The real Actions screen, Jot service, action and entity services, and disposable SQLite profile run together. The journey proves immediate raw persistence, visible enrichment progress, success and malformed-model recovery, canonical entity linking, immutable source evidence, and close/reopen persistence; only the local model HTTP response is controlled.
remaining: Repeat through the packaged Electron IPC bridge and the selected production local model.
notes: Guards entity and contextual to-do quality.

phase: 19 Pro entity, action, and service guardrails
manualTest: Contextual to-do lifecycle preserves source and entity links
steps: Move a sourced to-do through Open, Waiting, Done, Dismissed, Undo, entity filtering, entity detail, and a stale/deleted source target; relaunch between transitions.
expected: Status/counts persist, Undo restores correctly, entity and provenance stay attached, filters show the same record, and stale targets fail closed without opening another item.
proof: The real Actions UI, production action/entity services, and disposable SQLite profile now run together through Open, Waiting, Done, Dismissed, Restore, and toast Undo. Counts, entity filters, exact provenance, stale-target failure, delegated visibility, and close/reopen persistence follow the same canonical record.
remaining: Repeat the lifecycle through the packaged Electron IPC bridge and verify native notification entry points.

phase: 19 Pro entity, action, and service guardrails
manualTest: Entity correction and extension preserve the guarded record
steps: Using synthetic multi-source evidence, rename/retype, add/remove alias and fact, set photo, hide/unhide, merge, split/reassign an observation, re-synthesize, then relaunch and open every linked source/action.
expected: One canonical entity owns the right evidence and relationships, rollback/correction is possible, no self/noise duplicate is introduced, and every linked surface follows the surviving ID.
proof: The real screen extractor, dictation memory sink, meeting processor, manual-todo owner, Entities UI, correction services, disposable SQLite profile, and app-owned photo filesystem run together. Screen, voice, meeting, and manual evidence converges on one guarded entity; three contextual actions retain exact source provenance; rename updates every linked action projection atomically; and the corrected graph survives close/reopen. The suite also covers retype, aliases, photo import/replacement/rollback, hide/unhide, merge, observation reassign/unlink, stale-survivor rejection, and transaction rollback.
remaining: Add and integrate rendered fact add/remove and observation split controls; neither is invoked by the shipped Entity UI today. Then repeat through packaged Electron IPC.
notes: Entity is a protected cross-product unit; this is a release-significant guardrail.

phase: 19 Pro entity, action, and service guardrails
manualTest: Text-only model does not break capture memory
steps: Select an active text-only model with no vision projector, enable Pro capture, show a unique phrase, and wait for OCR/search/entity processing.
expected: Capture, OCR, text distillation, memory, and search continue; optional layout learning is skipped with readable diagnostics and no repeated vision error.
proof: Real active-model/projector admission and Pro capture guards are covered with OS/model-engine boundaries controlled.
remaining: Run the installed capture/OCR/search journey with a real text-only model and display.
notes: Do not classify this as only a chat attachment test.

manualTest: Entity graph renders and stays linked to canonical entities
steps: Open Entity Graph with synthetic people/projects/companies and relationships; search, focus, zoom, reset, rebuild, select a node, and return to entity detail.
expected: The graph remains responsive and legible, uses canonical entity IDs/relationships, rebuild does not duplicate data, and selected nodes open the exact entity.
proof: The real Entity Graph UI, Pro router, production entity services, and disposable SQLite profile run together. Search and focus use canonical IDs, zoom/reset stay operable, rebuild is idempotent, duplicates and polluted references are rejected, deletion clears stale selection, and Open record navigates to the exact surviving entity.
remaining: Repeat the graph journey through the packaged Electron IPC bridge and inspect layout legibility at release-scale data volume.
notes: Actual shipped Pro surface missing from the original CSV.

manualTest: Memory list and Master Memory lifecycle work
steps: Open Memories, search, select, delete a disposable memory, refresh, open Master Memory, copy it, regenerate master-only, then regenerate all summaries; interrupt and retry once.
expected: List/search/detail stay consistent, deletion is scoped, regeneration progress terminates, failures are recoverable, copied text is exact, and relaunch restores the completed result.
evidence: pro/renderer/screens/MemoryList.tsx; pro/renderer/screens/MasterMemory.tsx; memory DB tests cover adjacent stores.
proof: No dedicated installed or rendered integration journey was found for these controls.
remaining: Exercise production IPC, real local model, clipboard, persistence, and error states.
notes: Actual shipped Pro surface missing from the original CSV.

manualTest: Pro identity and learned-preference settings affect downstream behavior
steps: Change name/email/aliases, toggle proactive delivery, remove one learned preference, clear all learned preferences, relaunch, then ingest synthetic self/external mentions and request a proposal.
expected: Identity persists and filters self mentions everywhere, delivery follows the toggle, removals stay removed, proposals use current preferences only, and Core settings remain unaffected.
evidence: src/main/__tests__/settings-persistence.dbtest.ts; pro/main/__tests__/identity.test.ts; Pro settings and secretary preference integration tests
proof: Persistence and identity/noise rules have seam coverage; rendered settings-to-downstream behavior is not joined.
remaining: Drive registered Pro Settings sections through production IPC and verify real downstream entity/action/notification behavior after relaunch.

manualTest: Notification inbox management remains coherent
steps: Seed each notification type; filter, open, mark one/all read, delete one, clear all, switch routes, and relaunch.
expected: Unread counts and filters are truthful, actions affect only intended rows, targets remain exact, empty state is clear, and persistence matches the visible inbox.
proof: Typed target routing is covered; full inbox CRUD and persistence are not joined.
remaining: Exercise production notification store/IPC and every visible inbox action through relaunch.
notes: Actual shipped controls not explicit in the original CSV.

manualTest: Voice library controls and settings agree with native behavior
steps: Exercise Hold/Toggle/Both, engine, language, max-items and retention settings; import, search, play, retranscribe, and delete recordings; relaunch.
expected: Each setting owns the real controller behavior, library counts/search/playback are truthful, retranscription replaces correctly, deletion removes owned media only, and settings persist.
proof: Voice persistence, engine choice, import, retention, and rendered controls are exercised in separate seams.
remaining: Join all controls through production IPC, real helpers/audio, media server, and full relaunch.

manualTest: Clipboard search, filters, tags, previews, and deletion stay consistent
steps: Create text, rich text, image, and file clips; search, type-filter, tag/untag, keep one selected while new events arrive, preview each type, delete selected/non-selected rows, and relaunch.
expected: Results and selection remain stable, previews match stored bytes, tags persist through dedup, deletion is scoped, and no stale preview or wrong restore target appears.
proof: Store, OS clipboard types, dedup, selection behavior, and key screen interactions are covered in separate seams.
remaining: Join real poller/store/IPC events with all rendered controls in the installed app.

manualTest: Vault complete CRUD and file handling stay encrypted and scoped
steps: Search/filter and create, edit, reveal/hide, copy, reopen, export/download, and delete every advertised Vault type including a disposable file; lock and relaunch between actions.
expected: UI fields match type, ciphertext remains unreadable at rest, copies/exports are exact, file preview is safe, deletion is scoped, and no secret leaks into logs or recent UI after lock.
proof: Real KDBX crypto/filesystem CRUD and selected OS clipboard actions are covered.
remaining: Exercise every rendered type/control, file picker/export, locked-state cleanup, and installed-app logs.

manualTest: Local GGUF import admits only valid models
steps: Import a valid local GGUF, a truncated/corrupt file, a duplicate, and a path with spaces; activate the valid model and relaunch.
expected: Only valid bytes are copied/registered, duplicates are resolved clearly, invalid files create no phantom model, and the active imported model answers after relaunch.
proof: Model admission, filesystem integrity, and persistence are tested; the real picker/rendered import journey is not joined.
remaining: Use the installed file picker and a real supported/unsupported GGUF set.
notes: Actual Models control not explicit in the original CSV.

manualTest: Project knowledge enable, disable, and delete change retrieval exactly
steps: Attach two synthetic documents, disable one, query both facts, re-enable it, delete the other, relaunch, and query again.
expected: Only enabled, existing documents contribute citations; deletion removes owned chunks without affecting the project or other document; state persists after relaunch.
proof: Real extraction, chunks, SQLite, project filters, and prompt context are covered.
remaining: Drive all rendered toggle/delete controls through production IPC and full app relaunch.

manualTest: Artifact export and deletion are exact and scoped
steps: Create text and image artifacts, open gallery/project views, export each to a chosen path, delete one, switch conversations/projects, and relaunch.
expected: Exports match exact content/bytes and safe filenames, deletion removes only the selected artifact/file, no stale preview remains, and surviving artifacts reopen under the right owner.
proof: Persistence, exact image bytes, preview, and save boundary behavior are covered in separate seams.
remaining: Join rendered gallery/project actions with real native save picker and filesystem deletion.
notes: Actual chat/project controls not explicit in the original CSV.

manualTest: Chat voice mode records, transcribes, sends, speaks, and stops
steps: Enable voice mode, record a synthetic utterance, stop, inspect transcript, send, hear the reply, interrupt playback, disable voice mode, and relaunch.
expected: One transcript and one user turn appear, playback uses the selected TTS model, Stop releases mic/audio immediately, text mode remains usable, and mode/settings restore correctly.
proof: Permission recovery, UI lifecycle, TTS action, and audio engines are covered separately.
remaining: Join real microphone, STT helper/model, rendered chat, exact TTS worker, and audible output on the installed app.
notes: Critical full voice journey not represented by TTS-only rows.

manualTest: Command palette search and navigation preserve focus and target
steps: Open the command palette by keyboard from several screens, search seeded records, navigate results with arrows/Enter, use See all, press Escape, and reopen.
expected: Focus is trapped/restored, results are current and typed, Enter opens the exact record, See all preserves query/facets, Escape closes only the palette, and no keystroke leaks to chat.
proof: Focus trap and adjacent navigation are automated; complete typed-result routing is not joined.
remaining: Exercise all result types and focus/keyboard behavior in the installed app.
notes: Actual Core shell surface missing from the original CSV.

manualTest: Model catalog search, filters, detail, and reset stay coherent
steps: At 1280 and 1800 widths, combine modality tabs, text search, credibility, size, sort, RAM buckets, and use-case filters; open/close details; download/activate/delete; reset filters.
expected: Counts and sections remain truthful, no stale/duplicate card appears, detail reflects the selected model, actions stay adjacent and reachable, and reset returns the complete dense grid.
proof: Responsive density, focus, and representative model controls are automated.
remaining: Manually combine filters/actions against real catalog and installed models and inspect pixels.

manualTest: Connector lifecycle controls stay synchronized
steps: Add one stdio and one remote connector; test, enable/disable, sync with and without a query, inspect tools, force an error, reconnect, delete, and relaunch.
expected: Status and tools match the real connector, disabled connectors cannot run, sync is bounded, errors recover without blocking others, deletion removes owned secrets, and state persists exactly once.
proof: Real connector repository, encryption, stdio child, discovery, tools, timeout, and deletion run with remote boundaries controlled.
remaining: Drive every rendered control and real remote/OAuth provider through relaunch.

manualTest: Normal quit leaves no owned helper or capture resource
steps: After using chat, image, TTS, STT, gateway, media, meeting, dictation, clipboard, and capture, quit normally and inspect Activity Monitor, microphone/screen indicators, ports 7878/7879/8439, and mounted images.
expected: Every owned process, listener, timer, shortcut, audio/capture resource, and DMG mount is released; committed data reopens and no feature restarts before entitlement/settings allow.
proof: Core now has one idempotent shutdown owner for runtime engines, TTS, media, gateway, listeners, and Pro activation. Pro has one disposer for capture, meetings, dictation, clipboard, shortcuts, timers, tray, and console resources. Integration coverage proves reverse-order cleanup and failure isolation without skipping later owners.
remaining: Inspect actual host processes, permission indicators, shortcuts, ports, and mounted images after one full signed-app session.
